ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JILL AMEN, BEN SHEPPARD, JAKE ROYAL AND GENERAL BEVERAGE CONSULTING & MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION: DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR AN EVIDENTIARY HEARING TO DETERMINE IF A BOND IS APPROPRIATE IS TREATED AS A MOTION TO REQUIRE A BOND PER CORPORATIONS CODE 800(C) AND IS DENIED. THE REQUEST FOR A BOND IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANTS HAVE PROVIDED INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SATISFY THEIR BURDEN THAT THE THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT PROSECUTION OF THE DERIVATIVE CLAIMS WILL BENEFIT HOUSE OF JANE OR NEMA OR THEIR SHAREHOLDERS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JILL AMEN and JAKE ROYAL'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the eighth cause of action for fraud in the second amended complaint is OVERRULED. The fraud claim sufficiently alleges all the elements of fraud by intentional misrepresentation and fraud by concealment of information for which Ms. Amen had a duty to disclose. The fraud claim is not alleged against Mr. Royal.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS JILL AMEN, BEN SHEPPARD, JAKE ROYAL and GENERAL BEVERAGE CONSULTING & MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all of the causes of action in the first amended complaint is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the eighth and ninth causes of action and overruled as to all other causes of action: Defendants' request for an evidentiary hearing to determine if a bond is appropriate is treated as a motion to require a bond per Corporations Code 800(c) and is denied. Defendants have not cited, nor is the court aware of any, authority stating or suggesting that a plaintiff's failure to allege that he informed a derivately sued corporation of the ultimate facts of each cause of action or delivered a copy of the complaint bars the plaintiff from proceeding with his derivative claims. Where, as here, there is an allegation that a demand on the corporation to sue in its own name is futile, the failure to make the further allegation about informing the coporation of the ultimate facts of each cause of action or delivering a copy of the complaint is of no logical or legal significance and thus does not, by itself, deprive the plaintiff of standing to pursue the derivative claims. Fairly read, all causes of action in the first amended complaint that are labeled derivate claims are brought on behalf of both Huse of Jane and NEMA and thus there is no violation of CRC 2.112. The seventh casue of action for breach of fiduciary duty adequately alleges an Ahmanson direct claim for controlling shareholder oppression. The eighth cause of action for converston is deficient because it purports to be a direct claim for converson of "assets belonging to the Partnership," but the only assets that were alleged to have been converted belonged to House of Jane and NEMA. If the converted assets belonged to an unincorporated entity distinct from House of Jane and NEMA, that needs to be clearly alleged. If the converted assets belonged to House of Jane and NEMA, the claim must be a derivative claim, not a direct claim. The ninth cause of action for fraud is deficient since there are insufficient facts alleged as to how Mr. Franklin and Mr. Rosenthal relied on the alleged fraud. If plaintiffs can do so in good faith, they are given leave to file a second amended complaint which seeks to remedy the deficiencies in the eighth and ninth causes of action. The request for a bond is denied because defendants have provided insufficient evidence to satisfy their burden that the there is no reasonable possibility that prosecution of the derivative claims will benefit House of Jane or NEMA or their shareholders. Specifically, defendants have not provided sufficient evidence that the alleged converted assets of Houe of Jane and NEMA either were not converted or have no value. Moreover, nothing in section 800 requires that a trial court permit the introduction of live evidence and this court declines to do so. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
